name: Mahmud Hasan Khan, PhD
title: Professor
bio_raw: Dr. Mahmud Hasan Khan is a Professor in the Department of English and Humanities at ULAB. He has an MA in English literature from Jahangirnagar University and a second MA, in applied linguistics, from International Islamic University Malaysia. His first PhD was also from the same university with a focus on youth identity formation in a multilingual and multiethnic society. The thesis was titled, “The discourse of popular culture: How the Malay youth interpret the culture of Other.” Later he joined the University of Malaya in 2007, the leading public university in Malaysia, and worked there for five years. In 2012 he started his second PhD at Macquarie University, Sydney focusing on language policy debates in Malaysia. The title of his dissertation was: “Medium of instruction policy as a symptom of Malaysian nationhood”. He returned to Bangladesh in 2016 and joined ULAB and worked till 2021. In June 2021 he joined IUB as a Professor in the department of English and Modern Languages. In 2023 he became the Executive Director of Sasheen Center for Multilingual Excellence and the Center for Endangered Languages at IUB. Professor Khan re-joined ULAB in 2025 as a Professor of English at DEH. Specializing in discourse analysis, Dr. Khan works as an interdisciplinary scholar combining theories in policy studies, linguistic anthropology, and sociolinguistics to address the debates around identity formation and ideological contestations. He is one of the editors of the Routledge Handbook of English Language Education in Bangladesh (2021) and Constructing Identities in the Malaysian Media (University of Malaya Press, 2008). His recent publications include chapters in Routledge (2024) and Springer (2023) on medium of instruction, equity and social justice. He has published widely in the areas of identity formation and policy discourses in various journals and edited books.
stated_interests: Sociolinguistics, discourse analysis, conversation analysis, language planning policy.
